title: ModExp via Gidney-layout modmult (modMultInPlaceShor):  x -> a^x mod N  =  exponent-controlled chain of modular multipliers   [bits=2, anc = adder_n_qubits-based, dim 4*bits+6 = 14]
input: e = q0..q3 = exponent x (control bits);   w = q4..q17 = |1> data + Gidney workspace (anc = adder_n_qubits(bits+1)+1)
output: w data register = a^x mod N;  workspace restored.   each box = the verified modMultInPlaceShor (ModMult/ShorOracle), controlled by one exponent bit

blocks.label:
x a^(2^0)
mod N
ctrl e0

x a^(2^1)
mod N
ctrl e1

x a^(2^2)
mod N
ctrl e2

x a^(2^3)
mod N
ctrl e3
